description: Ontology for automated fraud hypothesis generation using the Kaggle Healthcare Provider Fraud Detection Analysis dataset. Covers provider-level fraud detection through analysis of inpatient claims, outpatient claims, and beneficiary demographics. The PotentialFraud column is the primary ground truth label. Multi-hop paths traverse: Provider → Claim → Beneficiary → ChronicCondition → DiagnosisCode → ProcedureCode → Physician → GeographicLocation → TimeWindow.

datasource.description: Provider fraud labels and derived risk features

Provider.ProviderID: Text | Unique identifier for each healthcare provider (hospital, clinic, or individual practitioner) who submits Medicare claims.
Provider.PotentialFraud: Boolean | Ground truth label — Yes if the provider has been flagged as potentially fraudulent based on Medicare investigation. This is the primary target variable and verification oracle.
Provider.TotalClaimsCount: Numeric | Derived — total number of claims (inpatient + outpatient) submitted by this provider. Unusually high counts relative to peers is a fraud indicator.
Provider.TotalReimbursementAmt: Numeric | Derived — total USD amount reimbursed to this provider across all claims. Outliers at provider level are the strongest single fraud indicator.
Provider.TotalDeductibleAmt: Numeric | Derived — total deductible amount paid by patients for this provider's claims. Unusually low deductibles with high reimbursements can indicate inflated billing.
Provider.UniquePatientCount: Numeric | Derived — number of distinct patients (BeneID) treated by this provider. Fraud providers often have abnormally high patient counts to justify claim volumes.
Provider.UniquePhysicianCount: Numeric | Derived — number of distinct attending physicians associated with this provider. Many physicians at one provider can indicate a ghost billing network.
Provider.AvgClaimDuration: Numeric | Derived — average number of days between ClaimStartDt and ClaimEndDt for this provider's claims. Fraudulent providers tend to have shorter durations with higher reimbursements.
Provider.InpatientClaimsCount: Numeric | Derived — number of inpatient (hospital admission) claims submitted by this provider.
Provider.OutpatientClaimsCount: Numeric | Derived — number of outpatient (visit-only) claims submitted by this provider.
Provider.FraudRiskScore: Numeric | Derived — composite risk score combining normalized reimbursement rank and claim count rank. Range 0-1; higher values indicate greater fraud risk.

datasource.description: Patient demographics and chronic condition flags

Beneficiary.BeneID: Text | Unique identifier for each Medicare beneficiary (patient). Primary foreign key linking claims to demographics.
Beneficiary.DOB: Date | Date of birth. Used to derive patient age and validate age-appropriate procedures.
Beneficiary.DOD: Date | Date of death, if deceased. NULL if alive. Claims submitted after DOD are deterministic posthumous fraud.
Beneficiary.Gender: Numeric | 1=Male, 2=Female. Used to detect gender-inappropriate procedures being billed.
Beneficiary.Race: Numeric | 1=White, 2=Black, 3=Other, 5=Hispanic. Used for demographic distribution analysis.
Beneficiary.State: Numeric | US state code of beneficiary's residence. Used for geographic fraud concentration analysis.
Beneficiary.County: Numeric | County code within the state. Finer geographic granularity than state.
Beneficiary.IsDeceased: Boolean | Derived — 1 if DOD is not null, 0 otherwise. Critical flag for posthumous billing detection.
Beneficiary.Age: Numeric | Derived — age calculated from DOB relative to dataset period (2009). Medicare beneficiaries are typically 65+.
Beneficiary.NoOfMonths_PartACov: Numeric | Months covered under Medicare Part A (hospital insurance). Range 0-12.
Beneficiary.NoOfMonths_PartBCov: Numeric | Months covered under Medicare Part B (medical insurance). Range 0-12.
Beneficiary.IPAnnualReimbursementAmt: Numeric | Total annual inpatient reimbursement amount for this beneficiary in USD.
Beneficiary.IPAnnualDeductibleAmt: Numeric | Total annual inpatient deductible paid by this beneficiary in USD.
Beneficiary.OPAnnualReimbursementAmt: Numeric | Total annual outpatient reimbursement amount for this beneficiary in USD.
Beneficiary.OPAnnualDeductibleAmt: Numeric | Total annual outpatient deductible paid by this beneficiary in USD.
Beneficiary.RenalDiseaseIndicator: Boolean | 1 if the beneficiary has chronic kidney/renal disease.
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_Alzheimer: Boolean | 1 if diagnosed with Alzheimer disease.
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_Heartfailure: Boolean | 1 if diagnosed with heart failure.
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_KidneyDisease: Boolean | 1 if diagnosed with chronic kidney disease.
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_ObstrPulmonary: Boolean | 1 if diagnosed with chronic obstructive pulmonary disease (COPD).
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_IschemicHeart: Boolean | 1 if diagnosed with ischemic heart disease.
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_rheumatoidarthritis: Boolean | 1 if diagnosed with rheumatoid arthritis.
Beneficiary.ChronicCond_stroke: Boolean | 1 if the beneficiary has had a stroke.
Beneficiary.ChronicConditionCount: Numeric | Derived — total count of active chronic conditions (sum of all ChronicCond_ flags). Range 0-11. Mismatch between condition count and billed procedures is a key fraud signal.

Claim.ClaimID: Text | Unique identifier for each claim. Primary key.
Claim.ClaimStartDt: Date | Date the claim period begins (first date of service).
Claim.ClaimEndDt: Date | Date the claim period ends (last date of service).
Claim.ClaimDuration: Numeric | Derived — days between ClaimStartDt and ClaimEndDt. Very short durations with high reimbursements = upcoding signal.
Claim.InscClaimAmtReimbursed: Numeric | Total USD reimbursed by Medicare for this claim. Primary financial fraud signal.
Claim.DeductibleAmtPaid: Numeric | USD deductible paid by the patient. Low deductibles with high reimbursements = inflated billing signal.

InpatientClaim.ClaimID: Text | Unique identifier for the inpatient claim. Subtype of Claim.
InpatientClaim.AdmissionDt: Date | Date the patient was admitted to the hospital.
InpatientClaim.DischargeDt: Date | Date the patient was discharged.
InpatientClaim.AdmissionDuration: Numeric | Derived — days between AdmissionDt and DischargeDt. Very short stays (0-1 days) with high reimbursements = strongest upcoding signal.
InpatientClaim.AttendingPhysician: Text | ID of the physician who supervised patient care during the hospital stay.
InpatientClaim.OperatingPhysician: Text | ID of the physician who performed surgery. NULL if no surgery.
InpatientClaim.OtherPhysician: Text | ID of any additional physician involved. NULL if not applicable.
InpatientClaim.ClmDiagnosisCode_1: Text | Primary ICD-9 diagnosis code justifying the hospitalization.
InpatientClaim.ClmProcedureCode_1: Text | Primary ICD-9 procedure code for the most significant procedure performed.
InpatientClaim.InscClaimAmtReimbursed: Numeric | Total USD reimbursed for this inpatient stay. Legitimate range $0–$20,000 depending on procedure; outliers warrant investigation.
InpatientClaim.DeductibleAmtPaid: Numeric | Deductible paid by the patient for this stay.
InpatientClaim.DiagnosisGroupCode: Text | DRG code classifying this inpatient case type for reimbursement purposes.

OutpatientClaim.ClaimID: Text | Unique identifier for the outpatient claim. Subtype of Claim.
OutpatientClaim.ClaimStartDt: Date | Date the outpatient service was provided.
OutpatientClaim.ClaimEndDt: Date | End date of the outpatient service period (often same as start for single-day visits).
OutpatientClaim.AttendingPhysician: Text | ID of the physician who attended the outpatient visit.
OutpatientClaim.OperatingPhysician: Text | ID of the physician who performed any procedures. NULL if none.
OutpatientClaim.OtherPhysician: Text | ID of any additional physician involved.
OutpatientClaim.ClmDiagnosisCode_1: Text | Primary ICD-9 diagnosis code justifying the outpatient visit.
OutpatientClaim.ClmProcedureCode_1: Text | Primary ICD-9 procedure code for the outpatient service.
OutpatientClaim.InscClaimAmtReimbursed: Numeric | Total USD reimbursed for this outpatient visit. Legitimate range under $3,500; amounts above are anomalous.
OutpatientClaim.DeductibleAmtPaid: Numeric | Deductible paid by the patient for this visit.

datasource.description: Physician network risk and fraud association features

Physician.PhysicianID: Text | Unique identifier derived from AttendingPhysician field. Same physician can appear across multiple providers.
Physician.PhysicianRole: Text | Role in the claim — Attending, Operating, or Other.
Physician.TotalPatientsAttended: Numeric | Derived — total distinct patients attended. Unusually high counts suggest involvement in high-volume fraud.
Physician.TotalClaimsInvolved: Numeric | Derived — total claims this physician appears in across all providers. Extremely high values indicate central role in a fraud network.
Physician.AvgReimbursementPerClaim: Numeric | Derived — average USD reimbursement per claim. High values indicate association with expensive/fraudulent claims.
Physician.UniqueProvidersWorkedWith: Numeric | Derived — number of distinct providers this physician has worked with. Physicians working across many providers simultaneously = organized fraud indicator.
Physician.FraudAssociationRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of claims involving this physician that belong to fraud-flagged providers. Range 0-1; values above 0.5 indicate predominantly fraudulent associations.

datasource.description: ICD-9 diagnosis codes derived from claim records

DiagnosisCode.DiagnosisCode: Text | ICD-9 diagnosis code string identifying a specific medical condition.
DiagnosisCode.DiagnosisDescription: Text | Human-readable description of the ICD-9 code (e.g., 4019 = Unspecified essential hypertension).
DiagnosisCode.FrequencyInFraudClaims: Numeric | Derived — count of times this code appears in claims from fraud-flagged providers.
DiagnosisCode.AvgReimbursementAmt: Numeric | Derived — average USD reimbursement for claims using this diagnosis code. High-reimbursement diagnosis codes are preferred by fraudulent providers.
DiagnosisCode.FraudRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of claims using this code that belong to fraud-flagged providers. Range 0-1.

datasource.description: ICD-9 procedure codes derived from claim records

ProcedureCode.ProcedureCode: Text | ICD-9 procedure code string identifying a specific medical procedure.
ProcedureCode.ProcedureDescription: Text | Human-readable description of the procedure code.
ProcedureCode.FrequencyInFraudClaims: Numeric | Derived — count of times this procedure code appears in fraud-flagged provider claims.
ProcedureCode.AvgReimbursementAmt: Numeric | Derived — average USD reimbursement for claims using this procedure. High-reimbursement procedures are commonly misused in upcoding fraud.
ProcedureCode.FraudRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of claims using this procedure that belong to fraud-flagged providers. Range 0-1.

GeographicLocation.County: Numeric | Numeric county code within the state.
GeographicLocation.StateName: Text | Human-readable state name derived from code mapping.
GeographicLocation.TotalProviders: Numeric | Derived — total distinct providers operating in this state.
GeographicLocation.FraudProviderRate: Numeric | Derived — proportion of providers in this state that are fraud-flagged. Range 0-1; high values indicate geographic fraud hotspots.
GeographicLocation.AvgReimbursementPerClaim: Numeric | Derived — average USD reimbursement per claim for all claims from this state.

TimeWindow.ClaimYear: Numeric | Calendar year of the claim start date.
TimeWindow.ClaimMonth: Numeric | Calendar month (1-12) of the claim start date.
TimeWindow.ClaimQuarter: Numeric | Quarter (1-4) of the claim start date.
TimeWindow.IsHolidayPeriod: Boolean | Derived — 1 if Q4 (Oct-Dec), 0 otherwise. Holiday periods may show different fraud patterns due to oversight changes.
TimeWindow.AvgClaimsPerDay: Numeric | Derived — average number of claims submitted per day in this period. Spikes indicate coordinated fraud bursts.
TimeWindow.FraudRateInPeriod: Numeric | Derived — proportion of claims in this period belonging to fraud-flagged providers.

relationtrees.description: Directed paths through the ontology graph. Each path enables multi-hop hypothesis traversal. Minimum 4-hop paths are required for non-trivial AmbientOn hypotheses.
multi_hop_example_paths: Provider → Claim → Beneficiary → ChronicCondition (4 hops): Are high-reimbursement providers billing patients whose chronic conditions do not justify the procedures?
Provider → Claim → Physician → Provider (4 hops): Are the same physicians appearing across multiple fraud-flagged providers — indicating an organized network?
Provider → Claim → DiagnosisCode → ProcedureCode (4 hops): Are providers billing high-reimbursement procedures for diagnoses that do not typically require them?
Beneficiary → Claim → TimeWindow → Provider (4 hops): Are deceased patients' claims being submitted after DOD, and which providers are responsible?
Provider → GeographicLocation → Provider → Claim (4 hops): Are geographically clustered providers showing correlated fraud patterns — indicating regional fraud rings?

axioms.name: Posthumous billing detection
axioms.rule: IF Claim.ClaimStartDt > Beneficiary.DOD AND Beneficiary.IsDeceased = 1 THEN Claim.FraudType = 'PosthumousBilling' AND Claim.VerificationMethod = 'Deterministic'

axioms.name: Reimbursement outlier detection
axioms.rule: IF Claim.InscClaimAmtReimbursed > Q3(InscClaimAmtReimbursed | same DRG) + 1.5 * IQR(InscClaimAmtReimbursed | same DRG) THEN Claim.IsReimbursementOutlier = TRUE

axioms.name: Physician multi-provider flag
axioms.rule: IF Physician.UniqueProvidersWorkedWith > 2 AND Physician.FraudAssociationRate > 0.5 THEN Physician.FraudNetworkRisk = 'High'
axioms.rationale: Threshold relaxed from >5 to >2 providers based on empirical observation that the Kaggle dataset's physician network is sparse. A physician working across 3+ providers with >50% fraud association is still a strong organized fraud signal. Original threshold of >5 yielded 0 flagged physicians on this dataset.

axioms.name: Procedure-diagnosis mismatch
axioms.rule: IF ProcedureCode.ProcedureCode is NOT in expected_procedures_for(DiagnosisCode.DiagnosisCode) THEN Claim.FraudType = 'ProcedureDiagnosisMismatch'

axioms.name: High-volume low-condition billing
axioms.rule: IF Provider.TotalReimbursementAmt > PEER_95TH_PERCENTILE AND AVG(Beneficiary.ChronicConditionCount) < PEER_MEDIAN THEN Provider.FraudRiskScore += 0.2

ground_truth_strategy.primary: PotentialFraud column (provider-level binary label) — direct verification oracle
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_statistical: IQR outlier detection on InscClaimAmtReimbursed per DRG group — flags claim-level anomalies
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_temporal: Rolling 4-week z-score on AvgClaimsPerDay per TimeWindow — flags burst patterns
ground_truth_strategy.secondary_deterministic: ClaimStartDt > BeneDOD — deterministic posthumous fraud, no statistical test needed

hypothesis_examples: Providers billing high reimbursements for patients with no matching chronic conditions are likely committing upcoding fraud.
Physicians appearing across more than 5 providers with a fraud association rate above 0.5 are central nodes in an organized Medicare fraud network.
Claims submitted after a beneficiary's recorded date of death indicate posthumous billing — the provider is billing for services that were never rendered.
Providers in geographic clusters (same state/county) with correlated FraudRiskScores suggest regional fraud rings sharing billing patterns.
Outpatient claims with reimbursements above $3,500 using high-fraud-rate diagnosis codes indicate systematic upcoding of outpatient services as inpatient.
